zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight於1478年出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾時，他的父親Robert Spencer18歲，母親Anne Pecke20歲。他於1502年與Christine Etheldreia Baker在英國英格蘭貝德福爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1532年2月5日在他的家鄉享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight于1478年出生在英国英格兰貝德福爾时，他的父亲Robert Spencer18岁，母亲Anne Pecke20岁。他于1502年与Christine Etheldreia Baker在英国英格兰貝德福爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1532年2月5日在他的家乡享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spenser
meanings: English: variant of   Spencer  .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: English:

occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Mylles,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Mills  .

variants.name: Mills
meanings: English: variant of Mill 1, with excrescent -s added in post-medieval times. Compare Mullins , from a French equivalent of this name, and see also Milnes .
English: either a variant of Miles , a variant of Mill 2, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, or Myhill , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: this is usually the English name, especially in Ulster, but elsewhere in Ireland it was also adopted for the Gaelic topographic byname, an Mhuilinn ‘of the mill’.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight was born in 1478, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Spencer, was 18 and his mother, Anne Pecke, was 20. He married Christine Etheldreia Baker in 1502, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 5 February 1532, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight est né en 1478, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Spencer, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Anne Pecke, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Christine Etheldreia Baker en 1502, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 5 février 1532, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight 1478 in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Spencer 18 und seine Mutter Anne Pecke 20. Er hat 1502 in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Christine Etheldreia Baker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 5. Februar 1532 im Alter von 54 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight è nato il nel 1478 nel Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Spencer, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Anne Pecke, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Christine Etheldreia Baker nel 1502, nel Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 5 febbraio 1532, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knightが1478年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert Spencerは18歳，母Anne Peckeは20歳であった。1502年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアでChristine Etheldreia Bakerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1532年2月5日に故郷で54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight(이)가 1478년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Spencer(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Pecke(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1502년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 Christine Etheldreia Baker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1532년 2월 5일 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight nasceu em 1478, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Spencer, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Anne Pecke, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Christine Etheldreia Baker em 1502, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 5 de fevereiro de 1532, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight родился в 1478 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Spencer было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Pecke, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Christine Etheldreia Baker в 1502 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 5 февраля 1532 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Spencer van Mylles, Knight nació en 1478, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Spencer, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Anne Pecke, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Christine Etheldreia Baker en 1502, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 5 de febrero de 1532, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años.
